item.title: Families of victims in Sunamganj to file case against local brokers
item.description: Families of the twelve victims of Sunamganj, who died in the Mediterranean Sea late Friday during a six-day suffering to reach Europe from Libya, are preparing to file cases against the local brokers for sending their family members abroad through a risky way....

item.title: Ex-CJ Khairul shown arrested in two July murder cases
item.description: Former chief justice ABM Khairul Haque was on Monday shown arrested in two murder cases linked to the July 2024 uprising, filed with Jatrabari and Adabor police stations in Dhaka....City & Local Guides

item.title: Iran, Israel trade strikes as US mulls Gulf ground assaults
item.description: Israel and Iran exchanged more missile fire Monday as concerns that the United States might escalate the Middle East conflict by launching ground raids against the Islamic republic’s Gulf islands sent oil prices soaring....

item.title: July attackers to be tried regardless of uniform, home minister tells JS
item.description: Home minister Salahuddin Ahmed on Monday stated that those who had carried out armed attacks against activists during the July mass uprising were criminals, regardless of the uniform they wore, assuring that all related cases would be thoroughly investigated....

item.title: Minister’s claim on measles immunisation contradicts official data
item.description: Health minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Husain’s claim that there had been no measles immunisation in Bangladesh over the past eight years contradicts the government data and international health agency records....

item.title: Most measles cases found in infants below vaccination age
item.description: A significant proportion of measles deaths and infections in Bangladesh have been reported among infants below nine months of age, the age at which the first dose of the measles vaccine is administered, as the number of cases and deaths continues......

item.title: Tk 24,359cr farm credit disbursed in 7 months
item.description: Agricultural credit flow rebounded in the current fiscal year as banks expanded lending to rural borrowers......

item.title: Japanese survey flags key risks to business in Bangladesh
item.description: Political and social instability, along with time-consuming tax and administrative procedures, remained major risks for Japanese businesses in Bangladesh, according to a survey......Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Repayment nears $3b in 8 months
item.description: Bangladesh’s foreign loan receiving and repayment have reached nearly equal level in the first eight months of the current financial year, 2025-26, according to the latest data released by the Economic Relations Division on Monday......

item.title: Bangladesh meets NZ, EU teams, discusses trade, investment
item.description: New Zealand has proposed initiating negotiations for a bilateral free trade agreement with Bangladesh to significantly expand trade and investment between the two nations......

item.title: No fuel crisis, market strained by panic buying, hoarding, minister tells JS
item.description: Energy power and mineral resources minister Iqbal Hasan Mahmud Tuku on Monday told the Jatiya Sangsad that there was currently no fuel shortage in the country while an artificial pressure in the market was being created mainly due to......

item.title: Ctg port regains ‘zero waiting time’
item.description: Chattogram port, the country’s main gateway for import and export trade, has regained the much-desired ‘zero waiting time’ with no ships now waiting at the outer anchorage......

item.title: BFIU issues new e-KYC guidelines for insurance, capital market entities
item.description: The Bangladesh Financial Intelligence Unit, the financial intelligence wing of the Bangladesh Bank, has issued comprehensive new ‘Guidelines on Electronic Know Your Customer (e-KYC)’......

item.title: Dragon fruit farming boosts Barind farmers’ income
item.description: Commercial cultivation of dragon fruit is rapidly transforming the agricultural landscape of the Barind region, offering new income opportunities and renewed optimism for farmers in Rajshahi and surrounding districts......

item.title: Bangladesh govt struggles to tame fuel demand
item.description: About 2.8 lakh litres of hoarded fuel oils have been recovered since Friday from different parts of the country, but still the government is struggling to check the high demand amid supply disruption of petroleum products because of the rising war in the Persian Gulf....

item.title: G7 ministers tackle economic fallout of Mideast war
item.description: G7 ministers and central bank officials gathered Monday to tackle the economic consequences of the war in the Middle East that has sent energy prices soaring and has triggered fears for the world economy......

item.title: Oil prices rise on Trump’s Iran threats
item.description: Oil prices rose as the Middle East crisis escalated with the entry of Houthi rebels into the war and as US president Donald Trump threatened to destroy Iran’s main export terminal......

item.title: Japan Foundation DG calls on DU VC
item.description: Director general of Japan Foundation in South Asia Sato Koji paid a courtesy call on Dhaka University vice-chancellor Professor ABM Obaidul Islam at his office on Sunday. ...City & Local Guides

item.title: Honey collection in Sundarbans begins tomorrow
item.description: The much-awaited honey collection season in the Sundarbans, the world’s largest mangrove forest, is set to begin from April 1, with honey collectors (mowal) already completing their preparations......

item.title: Rights denied in the hills
item.description: ON MARCH 7, 2026, members of the Bawm community in Bangladesh’s Chattogram Hill Tracts marked 700 days of detention without trial. Among those detained were women and children, arrested in connection with a 2024 bank robbery allegedly linked to the Kuki-Chin National Front. In the days leading up to International Women’s Day, human rights activists......

item.title: ‘Climate resilience’ is patriarchal myth
item.description: IN THE salt-scarred plains of Satkhira, the water carries more than the residue of encroaching seas; it carries the quiet imprint of policy failure. For women who walk kilometres each day beneath an unforgiving sun, the climate crisis is not an abstraction measured in parts per million. It is lived through the body — in the sting of saline water against skin, in untreated......

item.title: Geopoliticonomic cascades of Iran war
item.description: MODERN wars no longer remain confined to the battlefield. In the contemporary global system, military conflict radiates outward through financial markets, energy supply chains, maritime trade routes and alliance politics. A missile strike in one region can quickly alter inflation expectations, shipping insurance premiums and diplomatic alignments across continents......

item.title: Agriculture, emissions and ecological strain
item.description: IN THE fertile plains of Bangladesh, where emerald rice fields define both landscape and livelihood, agriculture has long stood as a pillar of survival. Yet beneath this green abundance lies a growing contradiction. The very practices that sustain food security are increasingly contributing to environmental degradation and climate change. As the country strives to feed over......

item.title: Fuel, fear and fiction of scarcity
item.description: THERE is a peculiar pattern in modern crises: the line between real scarcity and perceived scarcity blurs faster than policymakers can respond. Bangladesh’s current energy predicament sits precisely on that fault line. It is, at once, a crisis born of geopolitics and one manufactured — quietly, incrementally — by human behaviour......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: Human trafficking laws should be strictly enforced
item.description: THE recent deaths at the Mediterranean Sea suggest that the government’s effort is not much effective in preventing human trafficking. At least 48 migrants, mostly believed to be Bangladeshis, set off from Tobruk, Libya on March 21 towards Greece in a small rubber boat after each family had reportedly paid around Tk 12 lakh to traffickers. In the next six days, the......

item.title: Govt must strengthen ACC, anti-corruption efforts1
item.description: THE growing backlog of corruption cases that the Anti-Corruption Commission handles reflects a system in distress. With 8,330 cases pending with courts, the problem is no longer simply administrative. It is now institutional. What is also concerning is a steady decline in the disposal rate, which has declined from around 10 per cent to little more than 7 per cent in recent......

item.title: Rescue operation in Daulatdia bus plunging incident ends
item.description: Goalanda upazila administration announced the completion of the rescue operation after a bus plunged into the Padma River at Daulatdia ferry terminal at the upazila in Rajbari that killed at least 26 people....

item.title: UGC orders shutdown of unauthorised local, foreign univ campuses
item.description: The University Grants Commission of Bangladesh on Monday directed all unauthorised branch campuses and study centres of both domestic and foreign universities operating in the country to immediately suspend their academic activities....Political commentary Bangladesh

item.title: Two members of fraudulent gang arrested with fake visas
item.description: The Rapid Action Battalion on Monday arrested two suspected members of a fraudulent gang for allegedly embezzling money from people under the pretext of giving them jobs in foreign countries....

item.title: Data of 8.29 lakh Rohingyas shared with Myanmar for repatriation: FM
item.description: Foreign minister Khalilur Rahman informed the parliament on Monday that the Bangladesh government had forwarded detailed information on 8,29,036 Rohingya refugees to Myanmar in six phases, with the aim of facilitating their eventual repatriation....

item.title: PM’s letter of solidarity handed over to Qatar amir
item.description: Bangladesh prime minister’s foreign affairs adviser Humaiun Kobir on Monday called on Qatari minister of state for foreign affairs Sultan bin Saad Al Muraikhi, and handed over a personal letter from prime minister Tarique Rahman, addressed to the......

item.title: Travel ban imposed on Asif Mahmud’s former APS
item.description: A Dhaka court on Monday imposed a travel ban on Md Moazzem Hossain, assistant private secretary to the then interim government’s local government, rural development and cooperatives and youth and sports adviser Asif Mahmud Shojib Bhuyain, over graft allegations....

item.title: British Council launches global safeguarding toolkit for schools
item.description: The British Council has launched a global Safeguarding Toolkit to strengthen child protection practices in schools amid rising online and offline risks....

item.title: Two held over crushing farmer under excavator
item.description: Members of Rapid Action Battalion arrested two people including the prime accused in a case filed over the killing of a farmer who was crushed under an excavator while protesting against illegal pond excavation on arable land at Mohanpur upazila in Rajshahi......

item.title: AL leader shot in Kushtia
item.description: A LOCAL leader of the Awami League, activities of which are now banned, was critically injured after being shot by unidentified assailants at Mirpur upazila in Kushtia on Sunday night....

item.title: Discussion on genocide, Liberation War held in Rangpur
item.description: A discussion on the March 25 genocide and the War of Independence was held at the initiative of the Rangpur Regional Information Office at the Rangpur Adarsha High School in the city on Monday....

item.title: Missing teen found dead after 2 days in Moulvibazar
item.description: The body of a teenage boy was recovered at Srimangal upazila in Moulvibazar on Sunday night, two days after he went missing....

item.title: Four jailed for life for murder in Kushtia
item.description: A Kushtia court on Monday sentenced four men to life imprisonment for the murder of a farmer at Mirpur upazila in the district in 2019....

item.title: Brahmaputra erosion leaves 50 families homeless, over 100 at risk
item.description: Along the shifting banks of the River Brahmaputra, uncertainty has once again gripped riverine communities in northern Bangladesh, as relentless erosion over the past week has rendered at least 50 families homeless and pushed many more to the brink of displacement....Political commentary Bangladesh

item.title: CID recovers $3.6m smuggled thru cryptocurrency fraudulence
item.description: The Criminal Investigation Department said that it recovered $3.6 million, about Tk 44.14 crore, in cryptocurrency that was smuggled abroad through online-based fraudulent investment platform Metaverse Foreign Exchange....

item.title: Moving car catches fire on flyover at Tongi
item.description: A car caught fire while moving on the Dhaka-Mymensingh Highway flyover in Gazipur’s Tongi on Monday evening....

item.title: Primary decision on starting HSC exams from June 7
item.description: A primary decision has been reached to hold this year’s Higher Secondary Certificate and equivalent examinations from June 7....

item.title: Move to test DAGs, AAGs draws mixed reactions
item.description: Newly appointed attorney general Md Ruhul Quddus Kazal has asked 103 deputy attorneys general and 230 assistant attorneys general to sit for performance evaluation tests....

item.title: Opposition leader meets PM
item.description: Jatiya Sangsad opposition leader Shafiqur Rahman met prime minister Tarique Rahman at the latter’s office in the Jatiya Sangsad complex on Monday following the namaz-e-janaza of Speaker Hafiz Uddin Ahmed’s wife, Dilara Hafiz....
